{"key_event":"British occupation and Somali Youth League mobilization","reasoning":"British military administration after 1941 kept colonial coercive and administrative capacity in the moderate range, with expanded policing and controls, though the collapse of Italian rule and uncertainty over trusteeship prevented a stronger consolidated state. Civil society recovered from late-Fascist repression through the Somali Youth Club/League, pan-Somali nationalism, and mass mobilization such as the 1948 riots, but remained constrained by colonial rule, economic disruption, and internal divisions.","society_change":1.1,"society_power":4.8,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":5.3}
